CodeIssue.Title: Public Class Not Declared as Final
CodeIssue.Description: The class is not declared as final as per OWASP recommendations. It is considered best practice to make classes final where possible and practical (i.e. It has no classes which inherit from it). Non-Final classes can allow an attacker to extend a class in a malicious manner. Manually inspect the code to determine whether or not it is practical to make this class final.

CodeIssue.Title: Code Appears to Contain Hard-Coded Password
CodeIssue.Description: The code may contain a hard-coded password which an attacker could obtain from the source or by dis-assembling the executable. Please manually review the code:

CodeIssue.Title: Public Class Not Declared as Final
CodeIssue.Description: The class is not declared as final as per OWASP recommendations. CodeIssue.Title: Operation on Primitive Data Type
CodeIssue.Description: The code appears to be carrying out a mathematical operation on a primitive data type. In some circumstances this can result in an overflow and unexpected behaviour. Check the code manually to determine the risk.

CodeIssue.Title: Operation on Primitive Data Type
CodeIssue.Description: The code appears to be carrying out a mathematical operation on a primitive data type. In some circumstances this can result in an overflow and unexpected behaviour. Check the code manually to determine the risk.

CodeIssue.Title: Potential SQL Injection
CodeIssue.Description: The application appears to allow SQL injection via a pre-prepared dynamic SQL statement. No validator plug-ins were located in the application's XML files.

CodeIssue.Title: Class Contains Inner Class
CodeIssue.Description: When translated into bytecode, any inner classes are rebuilt within the JVM as external classes within the same package. As a result, any class in the package can access these inner classes. The enclosing class's private fields become protected fields, accessible by the now external 'inner class'.

CodeIssue.Title: Public Class Not Declared as Final
CodeIssue.Description: The class is not declared as final as per OWASP recommendations. CodeIssue.CodeLine: "import java.sql.*; public class TestClass { static String DBUSER; static String DBPW;"
